CABINET SECKETABIAT (Department of Statistics) New Delhi, the 8th June 1966 G.S.R. 975.—In exercise of the powers conferred by the proviso to Article 309 of the Constitution, the President hereby makes the following rules further to amend the Directorate of National Sample Survey (Recruitment to Class III posts) Rules, 1962, published with the notification of the Government of India In the Cabinet Secretariat, Department of Statistics No. G.S.R. 1728, dated the 13th December, 1962, namely :— (1) These rules may be called the Directorate of National Sample Survey (Recruitment to Class III posts) Amendment Rules, 1966. (2) They shall'corae Into force on the 1st April, 1966. (3) In the Schedule to the Directorate of National Sample Survey (Recruitment to Class III posts) Rules, 1962, for the entries against serial number IB, the following entries shall be substituted, namely:— -- 2 of 30 -- 10 II 12 Librarian One Non-gazet- ted Class III (noji- ministeiial) Rs. 210— io—290— 15—320— EB—is— 425- Does not arise Between 19—30 years Essential: Transferrer. (i) Degree of a recog- Age: No. nised university. Qualifica- (ii) Diptoma/Certifi- tion Yes. case in Library Science of a Uni- versity or any other Institution issued after one year train- ing. (iii) Three to five years practical experience in a library of stand- ing preferably in a Special/Research Lib- rary. Civ) Proficiency in the Scheme of Colon Classification, Desirable.: Knowledge in Biblio- graphical Documenta- tion and Reference Service*. Two years. Direct recruit- ment failing •which by transfer. Transfer Not ap- of persons plkable. working in similar or equi- valent grades from Central Govern- ment offices. [No. 2/3/65-Estt.n.] B. S. This amendment shall be deemed to have come into force with effect from (a) the 17th April, 1957, in so far as the transfer of the post to the Ministry of Iron and Steel is concerned, and (b) the 1st September, 1966, in so far as the revision of the pay of the post is concerned. MINISTRY OF LAW New Delhi, the \&th June 1966 G.S.R, 980.—In exercise of the powers conferred by clause (1) of article 290 of the Constitution, the President hereby mimes the following amendments in the notification of the Government, of IndA» in Vhe Ministry of Law No. G.S.R 17B2, dated the 23rd November, 1965, relating to the execution of all agreements under the "National Loan Scholarship Scheme", n a m e l y : ^ In the Schedule to the said notification after tfie entry 'North East Frontier Agency' in column (1) and the corresponding, entry in column (2) the following entries shall respectively be inserted,- namely:— (1) (2) Laccadlve, Minicoy and' Amindlvi The, Education Officer, L.M.A- Admlh- Island iatration, Kavaratti Island, C/o. MW/ASADHA 4, isw [PA*T IF- (Department of Legal Affairs) JVeu> DeUri. the 13th June 1968 O.SJC 981.—In pursuance of rule 2 and clause (a) of rule 8B of Order XXVH A the First Schedule to the Code of Civil Procedure, 1908 (5 of 1908) and In super- qession of this Ministry's Notification No. G.S.R. 1814 dated 11th December, 1964. the Central Government hereby appoints Shrl Romesh Chandra Madan, Assistant Standing Counsel, Land and Housing Department, Delhi Administration, to act air Government Pleader for the purposes of the said Order In relation to any suit or the legal proceedings by or against the Delhi Administration, In connection with the scheme of "Large Scale acquisition, development and disposal of land in Delhi". £No. P. 15(l)/66-J.J G. MINISTRY OF FINANCE (Department of Economic- Affairs) New Delhi, the 11th June 1968 G.S.R. 982.—In exercise of the powers conferred by clause (2) of Article 77 read with clause (1) of article 299 of the Constitution, the President is pleased to make the following rule, namely:— All documents necessary to be executed in exercise of the executive power of the Union In connection with the performance of the Loan Agreement, dated the 12th May, 1966, with the Government of Denmark for a food loan of 30 million Danish Kroners to the Government of India, shall be executed and authenticated on behalf of the President by any of the officers specified below:— Joint Secretary, Deputy Secretary or Under Secretary to the Government of India in the Ministry of Finance, Department of Economic Affairs. Ambassador of India in Denmark or the Charge d" Affaires of India in Den- mark or the First Secretary /Attache to the Embassy of India u> Denmark. Chief Accounting Officer/Assistant Chfef Accounting Officer, High Com- mission of India In London. Controller of Aid Accounts and Senior Accounts Officer in the Ministry of Finance, Department of Economic Affairs. Dated at New Delhi, this 17th day of June 1966. [No. F. 24(3)-EII/66.T M. K. NAIR. Dy. Secy. (Department of Revenue and Insurance) CUSTOMS New Delhi, the 2W7i June 1966 G.S.R- 983.—In exercise of the powers conferred by sub-section (1) of sec- tion 75 read with sub-section (3) of section 16,0 of the Customs Act, 1962 (B2 of 1962), the Central Government hereby makes the following further amendment in the notification of the Government of India in the Ministry of Finance (Depart- ment of Revenue) No. G.S.R. 576/F. No. 34/86/60-Cus.IV, dated the 28th May, 1960, namely:— In the Schedule to the said notification, after serial No. 318 and the entries relating thereto, the following shall be added, namely:— "319. These rules may be called the Customs and Central Excise Duties Export Drawback (General) Fifty-seventh Amendment Rules, 1966. 2- In the Second Schedule to the Customs and Central Excise Duties Export Drawback (General) Rules, I960, after Serial No, 253 and the entries relating thereto, the following shall be added, namely:— "254. Citrus Beverage Base—Fanta Orange." [No. 58/F. No. 254/1/66-DBK.) G.SJt. 986.—In exercise of the powers conferred by sub-section (2) of sec- tion 75 read with sub-section (3) of section 160 of the Customs Act, 1962 (52 of 1962) and section 37 of the Central Excises and Salt Act, 1944 (1 of 1944), the Central Government hereby makes the following further amendment in the Cus- toms and Central Excise Duties Export Drawback (General) Rules, 1960, namely:— These rules may be called the Customs and Central Excise Duties Export Drawback (General) fifty-eighth Amendment Rules, 1986. 2 In the Second Schedule to the Customs and Central Excise Duties Export Drawback (General) Rules, 1960, for Serial No. 217 and the entries relating there- to, the following shall be substituted, namely:— "217 Spectacle frames not otherwise specified and components and parts of such spectacle frames". [No. 59/F. No. 217/1/66-DBKL] R. C. MISRA, Dy. Sacy. (Department of Revenue and Insurance) CORRIGENDA New Delhi, the 25th June 1966 G S J L 987.—In the notification of the Government of India in the Ministry of Finance (Department of Revenue and Insurance) No. 84/66-Central Excises, dated the 30th April, 1966, published in F a r t II, Section 3, Sub-section (L) of the Gazette of India, dated the 30th April, 1966, at pages 801 and 802,— (i) at page 801, in the 7th line, for "14 Iron or Steel Products." read "14. KUMTAKAR, Under Secy. -- 7 of 30 -- tizo THE GAZETTE OF INDIA: JUNE 25", I96&/A5ADHA f, IfSS [PvUrt* D-^ DEPARTMENT OF COMMUNICATIONS (Posti & Telegraphs Board) New Delhi, the 15th June 1966 G.S.R. 988.—In exercise of the powers conferred by1 the proviso to Article 309 of the Constitution, the President hereby makes the following rules regulating the method of recruitment to the Telegraph Engineering Service, Class II in the Posts and Telegraphs • Department, namely:—. THE TELEGRAPH ENGINEERING SERVICE (CLASS II) RECRUITMENT RULES, , I860 1. Shott title and commencement.—(i) These rules m a y be called the Tele- graph Engineering Service, Class II Recruitment Rules, 1JJOT. (ii) They s6all come int* force on the date of their publication in the official Gazette. 2. Deflnltions.~In these rules, unless the context otherwise requires; (a> 'Department' means t h e Indian Posts' and Telegraphs Department. (b) 'Director General' means the Director General of Posts and Tele- graphs. (a) 'Government' means the Central Government. (d) 'P&T Board' means the Posts and Telegraphs Board. (e) 'Service' means the Telegraph Engineering Service (Class II). 3. Application.—These rules shall apply to the posts as specified in Column 1 of the Schedule annexed hereto. 4 Number, Classiflcatlott, a»d Scale «4 i » j , — T h e number of posts, their clas- sification and the scale of pay attached to them shall be as specified in columns 2 to 4 of the said Schedule. 5. Method of recruitment, probation ami pnmotixm,—The method of recruit- ment to the Service, the period of probation, and the lower grades from which promotion would be made, shall be as indicated in coiumna ft to 13 of the Sche- dule a n d Appendix I and Appendix II to these rules. ft DhiqpialiflHiaon.—fi) No person, who has more than one wife living, o* who, having a spouse living, marries in. any ease in which such marriage is void by reason of its taking place duelng the life-time of such, spouse, shall be eligi- ble for appointment to the said posts, and (ii) no woman, whose marriage is void by reason of the husband having a wife living at the fjme of such marriage, pr who has married a person w h o has a wife living; at the time of such marriage shall- be eligible for appofatatferit to the said posts: Provided that the Central Government may, if satisfied t h a t there are special grounds for so ordering, exempt any peison from the- operation of this rule. 7. Appointing authority ^-Appointment to the Service shall' b e made by the P&T Board or by airjp other authonity to whom pewter to make appointment to the Service may be delegated. 8 Power to rftlajtv-^Where the Central Government is of opinion that it ia necessary or expedient so to do, it may,'by Order, for reasons to be recorded in writing and in consultation with the Union Public Service Commission ' refax any of the provisions of. these rules with respect to any class or category of persons or posts! -- 8 of 30 -- SCHEDULE Reatdtmerd Rtdes for Telegraph Brtgvteervtg Service, Class II ia Deptt. of Contmumcatioiu (A. & T. As required under the rules. -- 9 of 30 -- 1122 THE GAZETTE OF INDIA: JUNE 26, 19W/ASADHA 4, 1838 [FAW JB— APPENDIX I Except, as otherwise provided in Appendix n in respect of recruitment t o the posts reserved for Ex-company employees of the Telephone Districts of! Bombay and Calcutta, recruitment to the Service shall be enth*ely by promo- tion on the basis of selection of officials indicated in paragraph 2 below, through a qualifying departmental examination. An approved list shall be prepared by a duly constituted Departmental Promotion Committee, by selection, from amongst the officials who qualify in the departmental examination. 2. Eligibility for appearing in the departmental qualifying examinations.^ Following categories of officials shall be eligible for promotion to- t h » Service:— (1) Engineering Supervisors. (ii) Wireless Supervisors, who would have been appointed as such, prior to the promulgation of these rules. (iii) Ex-company officials with the following designations— (a) Bombay Telephone District Engineering Supervisor (Power) Engineering Supervisor (Cable) Engineering Supervisor (Exchange) (b) Calcutta Telephone District Chief Exchange Inspectors Engineering Supervisors (Exchange) Chief Test Clerks Engineering Supervisor (Line) Engineering Supervisor (Installation) Engineering Supervisor (Cable) (c) Madras Telephone District Engineering Supervisor (Exchange) Engineering Supervisor (Line) Engineering Supervisor (Cable) 3 Departmental qualifying examination for promotion to the Service shall normally be held at least once in a calendar year, m the manner prescribed In Appendix III to these rules. 4. Admissibility for the departmental qualifying examinations.— The departmental qualifying examination shall be open to the eligible cate- gories of officials, referred to in para 2 above, provided that they fulfil the fol- lowing conditions:— fit Engineering Supervisors recruited and absorbed in_ that grade against the vacancies of a year, ordinarily, not less than five years prior to t h e year, of announcement of the said examination; fin Wirelfss Supervisors and ex-Companv officials, as indicated in paragraphs 2(ii) and JKiii) above, appointed as such, ordinarily not less than Ave years prior to the year of announcement of the said examination. 1 The elieibllitv lists of the candidates for consideration of the Departmental Promotion Committee, shall be prepared in accordance with the instructions, as m a y be lisued by the Government from time to time, -- 10 of 30 -- SEC. S(0] THE GAZETTE OF INDIA: JUNE S», 1908/ASADHA 4, 1888 II23t APPENDIX n Appointment to the Telegraph Engineering Service, Class II post* in the Telephone Districts of Bombay and Calcutta, which have been reserved lor ex- Company employees of the Telephone Districts of Bombay and Calcutta shall be made in the manner indicated below: 2. The officials belonging to the following cadres shall be eligible for consi- deration for appointment to the reserved posts in their respective Districts: Bombay Telephone District Engineering Supervisor (Power) Engineering Supervisor (Cable) Engineering Supervisor (Exchange) Calcutta Telephone District Chief Exchange Inspectors. Engineering Supervisors (Exchange) Chief Test Clerks Engineering Supervisor (Line) Engineering Supervisor (Installation) Engineering Supervisor (Cable). 3. Appointment shall be made entirely by selection on the recommendations of a duly constituted Departmental Promotion Committee from amongst t h e employees belonging to the cadres mentioned above, who qualify in a restricted' departmental examination, or those who have been specifically 'exempted by Government from appearing in the examination. 4. The rules for conducting the examination, and the syllabus therefor, shall be such as may be prescribed by Government from time to time* 5. Such of the officials who have put in a minimum of five years continuous- service in these grades shall be eligible to appear In the examination. 6. The eligibility list of the candidates for consideration of the Departmental Promotion Committee shall be prepared in accordance with the instructions, as- may be issued by the Government from time to time. APPENDIX III Standard and Syllabus of Examination (See para 3 of Appendix I) 1. The subjects of the examination will be: — (i) One paper in the specialised subjects of: (a) Telephoney or (b) Transmission or (c) Lines, Cables and Telegraphs. (ii) One paper of General standard In; (a) Transmission' and Lines, Cables and Telegraphs or fb) Telephony' and Lines, Cables and Telegraphs or (c) Telephony" and Transmlsalon'. -- 11 of 30 -- ^ I j j 4 T H * GAZrfrtk <tf INDIA: JUNE 25, lWASAttrfX 4, 1888 (PABT I t - (iii) One paper in Code Rules (with books). NOTES : — (a) If a candidate takes paper (i) (a), he will have to take paper (fl) (a), if he takes (i) (b) then he will have to take (ii) (b), and if he takes (i) (c), then he will have to take (ii) (c). (b) Each paper will carry 100 marks. 2. The standard and syllabus of the examination shall be such as the P&T Board •may specify from time to time. 3. The P&T Board may, in its discretion, fix such minimum qualifying marks in any or all of the subjects of the examination as it may deem fit. [No. 1081/11/49-STA.] I. P. GUPTA, Asstt. Director Genl., P. & T. MINISTRY OF FOOD, AGRICULTURE, COMMUNITY DEVELOPMENT AND COOPERATION (Department of Food) New Delhi, the 14th June 1966 GJ3JI. 989,*—Whereas resolutions have been passed' by the West Bengal •Legislative Assembly at its meeting held on the 25th March, 1966 and by the West Bengal Legislative Council at its meeting held on the 26th March, 1966 adopting t h * Warehousing- Corporations (Supplementary) Act, 1965 (20 of 1965), under clause (1) of Article 252 of the Constitution; Now, therefore in exercise of the powers conferred by the proviso to sub- section (2) of section 1 of that Act, the Central Government hereby directs that in the Schedul* t o t h a t Act, after the flgufes and words "8. Uttar Pradesh", the figure a n d words "9. West Bengal" shall be inserted. [No. 4517/M&W/9M-66/63-1 G.SJL. 9M.—In exercise of the powers conferred by clause (e) of section 2 rf>f the Warehousing Corporations Act, 1962 (58 of 1962), read with section 2 of the Warehousing Corporations (Supplementary) Act, 1965 (20 of 1965), the Central Government hereby declares the following commodities to be "notified commodities" i o r the purpose of the first mentiond Act, namely:— (a) Tea Chests. (to) Panels. (c) Battons. [No. r.26|/24763-SG.nj DEVAKI NANDAN GOYAL, Under Secy. (Department of Agriculture) New Delhi, the 16rh Juite 1066 G-S.R. 991.-—In exercise of the powers conferred by the proviso to article 309 ,of the Constitution, the President hereby makes the following rules regulating the method of recruitment to the post of Hindi Stenographer (non-gazetted) in tthe Ministry of Food and Agriculture (Department of Agriculture), namely:— 1. Short title and c o m m e n c e m e n t ^ - ^ ) These rules may be called the Ministry ,of Food »nd Agriculture (Hindi Stenographer Recruitment) Rules, 1966. -- 12 of 30 -- SEC, 8(1)] THE GAZETTE OF JNDIA j JUNE 25*. 1966/ASAJ>HA. 4, 1888 j i 2 ^ (2) They shall come into force on the date of their publication In the Official Gazette. 2. Classification and scale of pay*—The classification of the post and the scale of pay attached thereto shall be as specified in columns 3 and 4 of the Schedule hereto annexed. 3. Method of Recruitment, Age limit, Qualifications, etc.—The method of recruitment to the said post, age limit, qualifications and other matters relating thereto shall be as specified in columns 6 to 13 of the schedule aforesaid: Provided that the upper age limit prescribed for direct recruitment may be relaxed in the case of candidates belonging to the Scheduled Castes, Scheduled Tribes and other special categories of persons in accordance with the general orders issued by the Central Government from time to time. 4. Disqualification.—(a) No person, who has more than one wife living or who, having a spouse living, marries in any case in which auch marriage is void by reason of its taking place during the life time of such spouse, shall be eligible for appointment to the said post; and (b) No woman, whose marriage is void by reason of the husband having a wife living at the time of such marriage or who has married a person who has a wife living at the time of such marriage, shall be eligible for appointment to the said post. II/2(520)/60 dated the 21st April, 1965, namely:— 1. (1) These rules may be called the Planning Commission (Computor Grades I and II—Non-Secretariat) Recruitment (Amendment) Rules, 1966. These rules may be called the Rubber (Second Amendment) Rules, 1966. 2. In the Rubber Rules, 1955,— (a) in rule 27. in clause (ii) of sub-rule (4). for the words "and revoke them", the words "suspend or revoke such licenses or restore any licence so suspended;" shall be substituted; (b) after rule 43A, the following rule shall be inserted, namely:— "43B. Declaration regarding inter-State transport of rubber, (1) No person shall transport or cause to be transported rubber from one State to another State without a Declaration in Form N, (2) On demand bv any officer of the Board authorised in that behalf by the Chairman of the Board, or any officer of thf« Central or State Government authorised in that behalf by that Government. the person transporting rubber and the consignee shall produce the Declaration."; (c) in the First Schedule— (i) in Form B. items 3, 4, 5, 6. 7, 8 and 9 shall be renumbered as 4, 5. 6, 7, 8, 9 and 10, respectively, and after the items have been so renumbered, the following item shall be inserted, namely:— "3, Address in full of the place of storage."; (ii) in Form C, after condition (3), the following condition shall tte inserted, namely:— "(4) The licence shall be valid only in resnect of the place of business and the place of storage mentioned in the application for the licence"; (111) after Form M. the following Form shall b * inserted, namely:— -- 22 of 30 -- SEC. Secy. -- 28 of 30 -- SEC. 3(i)] THE GAZETTE OF INDIA: JUNE 25, 1960/ASADHA 4. 1888 (Department of Supply and Technical Development) New Delhi, the 15th June 1%'ti G.S.R. 1002.—In exercise of the powers conferred by the proviso to article JOy ol the Constitution the President Hereby makes the following rules regulating the recruitment to the post of Development Officer (Medical Instruments) in tne Directorate General ol Technical Development, namely:— 1. Short title and Commeneemen.t,— (1) These rules may be called the Director- ate General of Technical Development, Development Officer (Medical Instru- ments' Kecruiiment Rules, 19ti6. \2) They shall come into iorce on the date of publication in the OiflciaJ Gazette. 2. Application.—These rules shall apply for recruitment to the post specified in column 1 of the Schedule hereto annexed. 3. Number, Classification and scale of pay.—The number of posts, its classifica- tion and scale of pay attached thereto shall be as specified in columns 2, 3 and •i of the said Schedule. 4. Method of recruitment, age limit and other matters.—The method of recruit- ment, age limit, qualillcations and other matters connected therewith shall be as specmed in columns 5 to 13 ot the said Schedule. 5. Liability to serve in Defence Services or posts connected with Defence of India.—Any person appointed to the post specified in the said Schedule, shall, if so required, be liable to serve in any Defence Service or posts connect- ed with the Defence of India, for a period of not less than four years, including the period spent on training, if any;— Provided that .such person, Hi) shall not be required to serve as aforesaid after the expiry of ten years from the date of such appointment. (ii) shall not ordinarily be required to serve as aforesaid after attaininc the age of forty five years. 6. Power to relax.—Where the Central Government is of opinion that it is necessary or expedient so to do, it may, by order and for reasons to be record- ed m writing end after consultation with the Union Public Service Commis sion relax any of the provisions of these rules, with respect to any class or cate- gory of persons. 7. Disqualification.—(a) No person who has more than one wife living or who having a spouse living, marries in any ease in which such marriage is void bv reason of its taking place during the life time of such spouse, shall be eligible for appointment to the post and (b) no woman, whose marriage is void bv reason of the husband having a wife living at the time of such marriage or who has married a person who has a wife living at the time of such marriage shall be eligible for appointment to the post: Provided that the Central Government may, if satisfied that there are suffici- ent grounds for so ordering, exempt any person from the operation of this rule, -- 29 of 30 -- SCHHXJXE Recndanent Ride), for ike p*st of E/etvlopftieni Officer in Directorate Gerteral of Technical Development {Medical InstrutnenU) Ministry of Supply, Technical Development and Materials Planning Name of post i Development Offi- cer (Medical Ins- truments).
